metadata.title: One Wordmark, Every Move Remotion Knows.
metadata.description: A technique-showcase reel animating the HUMANITARIANS AI wordmark every way Remotion can — 16:9 landscape edition. One wordmark (1060×133), 19 techniques, for review: which moves earn their beat?

beats.narration_text: Hey, this is Liam, in for Bear. One wordmark. Nineteen techniques. We're going to run the HUMANITARIANS AI logo through every motion move Remotion knows, back to back, so you can judge which ones deserve to live.
Letter Cascade. Each glyph springs in left to right, staggered. Watch whether the delay reads as rhythm or just lag.
Word Slam. HUMANITARIANS lands heavy, then AI hits with a screen shake. Judge the punch — does it earn the drama?
Draw-On Glyphs. evolvePath traces each outlined letter, then the fill floods in. Works best on thick strokes — watch the corners.
Baseline Wave. A traveling sine pushes each letter up and down in sequence, then they settle. The question is whether the settle feels earned.
Tracking Breathe. Letter-spacing expands to wide, contracts back to spec. One inhale, one exhale. Clean or restless — you decide.
Typewriter. Glyphs appear one per tick with a terracotta caret. Classic. The caret color is the brand's one accent — earned here.
Mask Wipe. A clip-path sweeps left to right, revealing the full-size marquee word. Then reversed. Two directions, one beat.
Highlight Sweep. A light band passes across the ink, once. Watch whether it reads as gloss or glitch.
Ligature Spotlight. The leading H mark and trailing AI diagonal counter-rotate while the plain letters dim to twenty percent. The two custom marks get the frame.
Per-Letter Flip. Each glyph card-flips on its X axis in sequence. Satisfying or seasick — watch the middle of the word.
Scale Focus. Camera pushes from the full word into a single glyph and back out. The AI ligature is the target. Does the zoom land on the right thing?
Blur Depth. Rack focus front to back across the word — each glyph sharpens in sequence. The effect reads best on the full wordmark.
Color Interpolation. Ink sweeps to terracotta and back, letter by letter. This is a treatment beat, not brand law — the palette violation is intentional and temporary.
Elastic Physics. The full word drops in, squashes on impact, rebounds as a unit. Spring config is everything here — too stiff and it reads hard, too loose and it reads cheap.
Glitch Slices. Horizontal slices offset across the word, then snap clean. The resolution should feel like a correction, not a decoration.
Kinetic Stack. The stacked-break layout — HUMANITARIANS over AI — ripples line against line in opposing directions. Portrait-native and uses the full frame height.
Marquee Loop. Full-size wordmark tracks horizontal at three speeds: slow, medium, fast. Cut on the beat. The speed jump is the edit.
Assembly. Every glyph flies in from a scattered position and rotation to land in its slot. The chaos-to-order arc. Watch whether the landing reads as arrival or collapse.
Exit Family. Three quick exits back to back: letter-drop, blur-out, wipe-close. The word leaves three ways. Which one you'd actually cut to black on — that's the judgment.
Now you have the receipts. Take this prompt, run it on your own wordmark, and flag the two or three moves that actually serve the brand.
